title: fumaria mirabilis subsp. faurei
intro: Fumaria mirabilis subsp. faurei, also known as a subspecies of the greater celandine plant, is a flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate liver and gallbladder function, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Fumaria mirabilis subsp. faurei are thought to include its potential to aid in the treatment of various liver and gallbladder disorders, as well as its possible role in reducing inflammation and improving overall well-being. Fumaria mirabilis subsp. faurei can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions.
